data.title: US Guided Interscalene Block Compared With Sedation for Shoulder Dislocation Reduction in the ER
data.brief_summary: Shoulder dislocation is the most common joint dislocation presented to the emergency room (ER) and reduction by medical team is always needed. Shoulder dislocation and reduction are often very painful and require some form of sedation, pain relief and muscle relaxation for reduction maneuvers. Several sedation protocols for reduction maneuver are described in the literature, and each institution is guided by its own protocol to optimize patient comfort and safety. At the Tel Aviv Medical Centre
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1\. Patients (ASA SCORE I-III) without acute cardio-pulmonary decompensation, who arrive to the ER with shoulder dislocation for reduction maneuver.

Exclusion Criteria:

1. Unconscious patient
2. Patient refusal/unable to give informed consent
3. Patients with acute cardio-pulm
endpoints: Length of stay measured in minutes from the beginning of shoulder dislocation reduction procedure until the subject is ready for discharge from the ER according to the physician decision
